Heat waves, rising temperatures can worsen Multiple Sclerosis symptoms, warns senior Amritsar-based neurologist
Rising heat waves across India are not only causing dehydration and heat strokes in the general population, but are also quietly worsening symptoms for people living with Multiple Sclerosis (MS), turning everyday weather into a serious neurological challenge.
Multiple Sclerosis is a chronic autoimmune condition in which the body’s immune system mistakenly attacks the protective covering of nerves in the brain and spinal cord (Image: Pexels)
